entities.raw_quote: ...tamen debes habere pugillarem, et scribereomnia que tibi dicet.

entities.name: CLM 849 (Munich Handbook)
entities.raw_quote: The manuscript is from the first half of the fifteenth century, of unknown provenence... It is on paper...

entities.name: No. 1 (experiment for liberal arts knowledge)
entities.raw_quote: No. 1. For gaining knowledge of the liberal arts (fols 3r–5v)...

entities.name: No. 2 (experiment for causing loss of senses)
entities.raw_quote: No. 2. For causing a person to lose his senses (fols 6r–7v)

summary: This chunk presents detailed analysis and transcription of ritual No. 1 from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), a 15th-century necromantic manual, focusing on how to conjure a master spirit for acquiring liberal arts knowledge through dream incubation. It lists multiple spirit names—both infernal (Apolin, Maraloth, Berith, Sathan, Belzebuc, etc.) and divine/angelic (Dobel, Vriel, Sabaoth, Tetragramaton, Adonay, Perarabiel, etc.)—and describes the ritual circle, blood-written inscriptions, protective cloth, sword, and writing tablet used. The text reveals a hybridized conjuration technique blending liturgical invocations with coercive demon summoning.
key_passages: Apolin, Maraloth, Berith, ego, talis, vos exorcisoa et coniuro ex parte dei omnipotentis, qui vos vestra eleccione iussit antra subire profundi...

...per hec sancta nomina quorum virtute ligamini – scilicet Dobel, Vriel, Sabaoth, Semoni, Adonay, Tetragramaton, Albumay, Siloth, Moreth, Sadabin, Rodobel, Domiel, Perarabiel,c Alatuel, nominem, nominam,d [et] Vsobel...

O Apolin, Maraloch, Berith, Sathan, Beliath, Belzebuc, [et] Lucifer, supplico vobis vt precipiatis magistro – in eo [loco] (nominando eius nomen) – vt ipse debeat venire cras ante solis ortum ad me...
...scribi debet per te cum predicto sangwine... scribe in dicto circulo nomen eius scriptum per te...
In sompno apparent tibi tres maximi reges cum famulis innumeris, militibus et peditibus, inter quos est eciam quidam magister apparens...
